proposals.evidence: The DB text has scrambled quoted text from the district court's oral ruling in paragraph 33. The phrase 'get the is transcripts' contains an erroneous 'is', and the phrase 'This problematic.' is missing the required phrase 'is something that you have indicated is'. Additionally, the words 'is something that you have indicated So' appear out of order at the end of the quote. The authoritative PDF source shows the correct text with proper word order and grammar: 'get the transcripts. This is something that you have indicated is problematic...responsibilities here. So the facts'. The error appears to be an OCR/text extraction defect where portions of the quote were rearranged or duplicated incorrectly.
proposals.note: This is a high-confidence, high-risk fix involving changes to the substance of a quoted judicial ruling. The change corrects a clear ingestion error that scrambled the text sequence and removed required language, but the fix is verified against the authoritative PDF source.

proposals.old_exact: if that individual: . . . .
proposals.new_exact: if that individual: ....
proposals.evidence: Source PDF shows 'if that individual:\n            ....' (four consecutive periods with no spaces between them). This is the standard Bluebook format for omissions within quoted material. The DB text currently has '. . . .' (spaced periods, which is the Bluebook format for an ellipsis in running text, not within a block quotation). The statute text in [¶5] is a block quote from N.D.C.C. § 12.1-32-15(2)(b), and block quotations should use continuous periods without spaces for omissions.
proposals.note: Only the second ellipsis in paragraph 5 (after 'if that individual:') should be changed. The first ellipsis after 'becomes temporarily domiciled.' correctly uses '. . . .' format in both source and DB.

Flagging for awareness: source shows footnote indicators (superscript 1, 2, 4, 5) that would need to be verified as properly ingested into the opinions database.

proposals.old_exact: two years and the court's oral twenty-four days. The written order pronouncements reflected the amount of credit for time served as "3 years and 34 days[.]" followed that
proposals.new_exact: two years and twenty-four days. The written order that followed the court's oral pronouncements reflected the amount of credit for time served as "3 years and 34 days[.]"
proposals.source: PDF source 2025ND222.pdf extracted via pdftotext -layout; paragraph [¶3] end section describing credit calculation
proposals.evidence: The database text contains a mangled phrase: 'two years and the court's oral twenty-four days. The written order pronouncements reflected...' which is grammatically incoherent and introduces spurious words ('the court's oral' before 'twenty-four days'). The PDF source clearly shows: 'two years and twenty-four days. The written order that followed the court's oral pronouncements reflected...' The correction reorganizes the phrase to match the court's actual pronouncement and adds the missing word 'that followed' to clarify the temporal relationship. The text 'followed that' at the end of the DB phrase is also removed as erroneous. This is a split/join error where text was partially transposed during OCR/ingestion.
proposals.note: This error occurs in paragraph [¶3] describing the revocation hearing's discussion of credit for time served. The phrase is critical to the legal reasoning: the court awarded credit for 'two years' (on the original sentence) plus the time between arrest on the warrant and resentencing, totaling 'twenty-four days.' The mangled DB version makes the sentence grammatically incorrect and obscures the calculation.

proposals.old_exact: "Incarceration alone insufficient to establish abandonment, but incarceration with other factors such as parental neglect, withholding affection, failure to provide financial support, and lack of contact can support a finding of abandonment."
proposals.new_exact: "Incarceration alone is insufficient to establish abandonment, but incarceration with other factors such as parental neglect, withholding affection, failure to provide financial support, and lack of contact can support a finding of abandonment."
proposals.source: paragraph 20 block quotation from In re Adoption of I.R.R., 2013 ND 211
proposals.evidence: Quotation in DB omits the word 'is' ("insufficient" instead of "is insufficient"). PDF source shows the complete quoted sentence with 'is' present, making the grammar correct.
proposals.note: Missing auxiliary verb in substantive legal quotation. This is a high-confidence defect.

provides:

II

3. [T]
proposals.new_exact:
provides:

3. [T]
proposals.evidence: DB has erroneous 'II' section heading on line 51 between the text 'provides:' and the statute quotation '3. [T]he relationship...'. PDF source shows the heading is part of page layout, not part of the opinion text at this juncture.
proposals.note: The 'II' is a section heading that should appear only at its proper location in the opinion structure, not inserted before statute quotations.

flags.description: Section VII appears with only one paragraph, and that paragraph [25] contains signature lines (names of justices). This is structurally normal for opinions.
flags.location_quote: [¶25] Jon J. Jensen, C.J. Daniel J. Crothers Lisa Fair McEvers Jerod E. Tufte Douglas A. Bahr

coverage_note: Full opinion verified: caption, all four paragraphs (¶1-¶4), all citations and party names. No OCR character errors, split/join errors, digit errors, or substantive text divergences detected. Page break artifacts (0x0C and page numbers) in PDF are expected and handled by separate deterministic pass.

proposals.old_exact: Act as my own. . . . . . . . THE COURT:
proposals.new_exact: Act as my own. . . . THE COURT:
proposals.source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2025/2025ND206.pdf page 4, rendered as /tmp/p5.png; pdftotext extraction /tmp/src_2025ND206.txt lines 160-162
proposals.evidence: PDF shows 'MR. YOUNG: Act as my own. . . .' (4 dots with spaces) on one line, followed by '           ....' (4 dots without spaces) on the next line. DB incorrectly concatenates these as 8 dot-separated dots ('. . . . . . . .') instead of 4. This is a mishandling of the ellipsis in the OCR/ingestion process.
proposals.note: The court's Bluebook ellipsis form '. . . .' (4 dots with spaces between) is rendered with a line break and continuation ('.....') in the PDF layout. The DB transcription incorrectly expanded this to 8 individual dots instead of preserving the 4-dot form.

coverage_note: Full text compared. One substantive transcription defect found: ellipsis corruption in paragraph 12 (court testimony transcription). No OCR character errors, digit errors, citation errors, missing text, or structural issues detected. Caption formatting differs from PDF (labels moved to separate lines in DB) but this is layout, not content corruption."

Full opinion text examined including all paragraphs, citations, structural markers, and character-level comparison against authoritative PDF source. All 10 paragraphs verified. Section headings confirmed as I and III (II intentionally omitted in original). Page furniture artifacts properly handled.

proposals.old_exact:
State of North Dakota,

v.

Joshua Brock Martinez,

Plaintiff and Appellee

Defendant and Appellant
proposals.new_exact:
State of North Dakota, Plaintiff and Appellee

v.

Joshua Brock Martinez, Defendant and Appellant
proposals.evidence: The authoritative PDF shows the caption with party designations (Plaintiff and Appellee, Defendant and Appellant) placed on the same lines as the respective party names using right-aligned formatting. The database ingestion split these designations onto separate lines, creating an improperly fragmented caption structure. The PDF layout clearly groups each party name with its designation: 'State of North Dakota,                                   Plaintiff and Appellee' and 'Joshua Brock Martinez,                                Defendant and Appellant' on single lines with 'v.' between them. The DB version has the designations as orphaned lines, which is a caption formatting defect.
proposals.note: This is an OCR/ingestion defect where party designations were incorrectly split from their associated party names. The fix restores the semantic grouping shown in the authoritative PDF while maintaining reasonable spacing.
